sections.title: Field Notes
sections.subtitle: Document observations, hazards, controls, or corrective actions.
sections.name: meta.notes

sections.title: Housekeeping Inspection Checklist
sections.html:
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

INSPECTION CHECKLIST

Inspection Frequency Note: Regular inspections are required to ensure compliance with OSHA 29 CFR 1926.25 and WISHA WAC 296-155-020. Frequency should be determined by site-specific conditions and risk assessments.

Item #

Inspection Item

Pass

Fail

N/A

1

Work areas, passageways, and stairs are clear of debris, form and scrap lumber with protruding nails (per 29 CFR 1926.25(a), WAC 296-155-020(2)).

☐

☐

☐

2

Combustible scrap and debris are removed at regular intervals (per 29 CFR 1926.25(b)).

☐

☐

☐

3

Containers are provided for collection and separation of waste, trash, oily/used rags, and other refuse (per 29 CFR 1926.25(c), WAC 296-155-020(11)).

☐

☐

☐

4

Containers for garbage, oily, flammable, or hazardous wastes are equipped with covers (per 29 CFR 1926.25(c), WAC 296-155-020(11)).

☐

☐

☐

5

Garbage and other waste are disposed of at frequent and regular intervals (per 29 CFR 1926.25(c), WAC 296-155-020(11)).

☐

☐

☐

6

Floors, working surfaces, and passageways are free from protruding nails, splinters, loose boards, or openings (per WAC 296-155-020(2)).

☐

☐

☐

7

Cleaning and sweeping minimize air contamination with dust (per WAC 296-155-020(3)).

☐

☐

☐

8

Hoses and electrical conductors across aisles/passageways are covered or suspended to prevent tripping hazards (per WAC 296-155-020(4)).

☐

☐

☐

9

Sufficient safe clearances are maintained for aisles where mechanical handling equipment is used (per WAC 296-155-020(5)).

☐

☐

☐

10

Aisles and passageways are marked (per WAC 296-155-020(5)).

☐

☐

☐

11

Storage of material does not create a hazard; materials are stacked, blocked, or interlocked securely (per WAC 296-155-020(6)).

☐

☐

☐

12

Free access is maintained to all exits, fire alarm boxes, fire extinguishing equipment, and emergency equipment (per WAC 296-155-020(7)).

☐

☐

☐

13

Working and storage areas are free from accumulation of materials posing tripping, fire, explosion, or pest harborage hazards (per WAC 296-155-020(8)).

☐

☐

☐

14

Vegetation control is exercised (per WAC 296-155-020(8)).

☐

☐

☐

15

Lunchrooms, washrooms, and restrooms are kept in a clean and sanitary condition (per WAC 296-155-020(9)).

☐

☐

☐

16

Garbage cans in lunchrooms and restrooms are equipped with fitted covers and contents disposed of daily (per WAC 296-155-020(9)).

☐

☐

☐

17

Continuous clean-up of work area, including removal of rubble, scrap, boxes, crates, and excess material to trash disposal areas (per WAC 296-155-020(10)).

☐

☐

☐

18

All scrap lumber, waste material, and rubbish are removed from the immediate work area as work progresses (per WAC 296-155-335(3)).

☐

☐

☐

19

Floors and walkways are maintained in good condition, with loose/broken components repaired/replaced (per WAC 296-155-020(12)).

☐

☐

☐

sections.title: Housekeeping Inspection Checklist (continued 3)
sections.html:
REGULATORY AUTHORITY

OSHA: 29 CFR 1926.25 Housekeeping

WISHA: WAC 296-155-020 Housekeeping; WAC 296-155-335 Disposal of Waste Materials

AGC: Jobsite Safety Handbook Housekeeping

This form supports compliance with the above regulations. Retain completed forms for minimum 3 years per 29 CFR 1904.33 and WAC 296-27.
